question: What is Al-Fardan Q9?
answer: Al-Fardan Q9 is the institutional digital-asset division of the Al-Fardan Group, offering staking, lending, custody, and OTC execution to family offices and institutional investors across the GCC. The Al-Fardan name has over 50 years of financial-services heritage — Al-Fardan Exchange has been CBUAE-regulated since 1971. Al-Fardan Q9 launched in 2023.

question: Who owns Al-Fardan Q9?
answer: Al-Fardan Q9 is a trading name of the Al-Fardan Group, operating through legal entities Alfardan Holdings Limited (DIFC Reg #5605) and Alfardan PTC Holding Limited (DIFC Reg #5598). The broader group has over 50 years of financial-services heritage in the GCC.

question: Where is Al-Fardan Q9 located?
answer: Global headquarters: The Onyx Tower 2, The Greens, Sheikh Zayed Road, Dubai, United Arab Emirates. Registered in the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC).

question: Is Al-Fardan Q9 regulated?
answer: Yes. Al-Fardan Q9 operates with VARA-licensed infrastructure via Fuze (Morpheus Software Technology FZE, License VL/23/10/002) — our VARA-licensed infrastructure partner. We are DIFC-registered, and Al-Fardan Exchange has been CBUAE-regulated since 1971. We also maintain SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 27017, and ISO 9001 certifications, all audited independently every year.

question: What services do you offer?
answer: Four institutional services: (1) Custody — Fireblocks MPC cold storage with $250M Lloyd's insurance; (2) Staking — 7 networks (ETH, SOL, POL, ADA, DOT, AVAX, ATOM) with no lock-up on the CRM side; (3) OTC Desk — 16+ digital assets, $100K minimum ticket, settlement in 8 currencies (AED, SAR, KWD, BHD, QAR, OMR, USD, EUR); (4) Lending — Sharia-compliant Murabaha credit lines up to 75% LTV, backed 1:1 by BTC or ETH.

question: What is your relationship with Fuze?
answer: Fuze (Morpheus Software Technology FZE) is our VARA-licensed infrastructure partner — VARA Licence VL/23/10/002 issued 17 November 2023. A formal Memorandum of Understanding was signed between Al-Fardan Exchange L.L.C. and Fuze on 10 December 2023, formalising strategic collaboration on digital-asset custody, execution, and settlement under UAE regulatory guidelines. Signatories: Hasan Fardan Al Fardan (CEO, Al-Fardan Exchange), Mohammed Ali Yusuf (Co-founder & CEO, Fuze), and Khalid Al Fardan (Co-founder & CEO, Al-Fardan Q9). The MoU was announced publicly in Gulf News. Al-Fardan Q9 is the digital platform that emerged from the Al-Fardan Exchange ↔ Fuze collaboration.

question: How long has Al-Fardan been in business?
answer: The Al-Fardan name has over 50 years of financial-services heritage in the GCC. Al-Fardan Exchange has been CBUAE-regulated since 1971. Al-Fardan Q9 (the digital-asset division) launched in 2023.

question: Is Al-Fardan Q9 Sharia-compliant?
answer: Yes. All products are reviewed by our independent Sharia Supervisory Board, chaired by Sheikh Dr. Tariq Al-Mahrouqi — a member of the AAOIFI (Accounting and Auditing Organization for Islamic Financial Institutions) Sharia Board. Lending is structured as Murabaha (disclosed cost, not interest/riba). Staking rewards are compensation for network security services, not interest. Full Sharia reports are available on our website under Shariah Reports.

question: How are my assets secured?
answer: Fireblocks MPC-CMP (Multi-Party Computation, Configurable Multi-Protocol) cold storage with 95% air-gapped custody. Private keys distributed across geographically separated hardware security modules (HSMs) in multiple Tier IV data centers across separate jurisdictions. 5% in warm wallets for operational liquidity. Same institutional-grade custody used by the world's largest banks and exchanges. Each user gets a dedicated Fireblocks vault. 2FA required for all withdrawals.

question: Are my assets insured?
answer: Yes. $250 million digital-asset crime + custody insurance policy through Lloyd's of London (Policy SY-2025-49881), underwritten by Apollo, Arch, Ark, Ascot, and Aspen syndicates. The policy covers custody-layer loss, theft from Fireblocks infrastructure, and most insider-fraud scenarios. The Lloyd's policy also covers Technology E&O scenarios (e.g. Fireblocks downtime). Does NOT cover individual account takeover where the attacker had your password + 2FA.

question: Do you have SOC 2 or ISO certifications?
answer: Yes — SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001 (information security), ISO 27017 (cloud security), and ISO 9001 (quality management). All independently audited every year. Documentation available under NDA — contact compliance@alfardanq9.com.

question: What is your Proof of Reserves?
answer: 215% reserve coverage ratio. Our Institutional Vault page on the website shows $2.55B total verified across 18 wallets. Full proof-of-reserves documentation with the specific wallet addresses available under NDA.

question: Is custody segregated / do you rehypothecate?
answer: Yes, fully segregated. Every client's assets are held in independent on-chain addresses with independent audit trails. Strict no-rehypothecation policy — your assets are yours, never lent out to anyone else. In a bankruptcy scenario, client assets don't become part of the estate — they're held in trust, always.

question: What happens if Al-Fardan goes bankrupt?
answer: All client assets are held in dedicated Fireblocks MPC vaults, legally segregated from Al-Fardan operational assets. Even in a bankruptcy scenario, your crypto does NOT become part of the bankruptcy estate — it's held in trust for you. The trustee's role would be to transfer Fireblocks policy-signing rights to a successor custodian, after which you regain access normally. Segregation + Fireblocks structure is specifically designed to prevent FTX-style commingled-funds catastrophe.

question: What's your uptime SLA and reliability?
answer: 99.95% validator uptime SLA, backed by redundant infrastructure and 24/7 monitoring. Zero slashing events since inception. Our OTC desk and institutional support are available 24/7/365. Matching engine provides <50ms average execution latency for institutional trades.
